items.q: Why must students distinguish the narrator from the author?
items.correct: The narrator is a constructed voice within the text; the author is the real person who created it. Conflating them misreads irony, unreliable narration and persona.
items.misconception: The narrator's views are always the author's views.
items.random_wrong: The narrator and author are different words for the same thing.
items.why: Students attribute everything a first-person narrator says to the author. This collapses irony — a novel like 'Lolita' cannot work if you take Humbert as Nabokov.

items.correct: The claim that judging a text by what the author 'intended' is fallacious — the text's meaning lies in the work itself, not the author's stated or guessed intentions.
items.misconception: It means authors can never make mistakes when writing.
items.random_wrong: It means it is wrong to write fiction.
items.why: Students assume 'author intent' settles meaning. Wimsatt and Beardsley argued it doesn't — meaning is a public property of the text, not a private fact about the author.

items.correct: A novel tracing a protagonist's moral and psychological development from youth to adulthood — typically involving formative experiences and a clearer sense of self.
items.misconception: Any novel with a young protagonist is a bildungsroman.
items.random_wrong: A bildungsroman is a novel about constructing buildings.
items.why: The focus must be on inward growth and development — having a young protagonist isn't enough. Students apply the term loosely to any story featuring a child or teenager.

items.q: Barthes's 'The Death of the Author' was published in:
items.why: It's 1967. “1949” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Derrida's central term for the deferral and difference of meaning is:
items.why: It's Différance. “Defamiliarisation” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Iser's 'The Implied Reader' was published in (German):
items.why: The real figure is 1972. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
